findings.evidence: `TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:49-55` defines `Additive G ⧸ (Subgroup.square G).toAddSubgroup` and its `QuotientAddGroup.zmodModule`; `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/FreeModule/ModN.lean:25-30` defines `ModN G n := G ⧸ LinearMap.range (LinearMap.lsmul ℤ G n)` and its `Module (ZMod n)` instance, whose `n = 2`, `G = Additive _` range is the subgroup of doubles/squares.
findings.fix: Define the quotient via `ModN (Additive G) 2` or provide the multiplicative square interface as wrappers around `ModN` rather than a second quotient/module construction.
findings.issue: `elementaryTwoQuotient` duplicates Mathlib's additive quotient-by-2 API: this is `ModN (Additive G) 2`, with the same `ZMod 2` module instance.

findings.evidence: `TauCeti/FieldTheory/SquareClassGroup.lean:35-47` already defines `SquareClassGroup K := Additive Kˣ ⧸ (Subgroup.square Kˣ).toAddSubgroup`, its `Module (ZMod 2)` instance, and `squareClass`; `TauCeti/Algebra/Group/ElementaryTwoQuotient.lean:49-66` repeats the same construction for arbitrary `G`.
findings.fix: Make `SquareClassGroup K` an abbrev/specialization of the shared construction, or add the necessary equivalence/compatibility in this PR so downstream code does not split across duplicate quotient APIs.
findings.issue: The new general API is parallel to TauCeti's existing square-class API for `Kˣ`, producing two names and two module instances for the same quotient.

summary: The PR introduces a new quotient-by-two API instead of reusing Mathlib's existing `ModN`, and it leaves TauCeti's existing square-class quotient as a parallel API for the same construction.
